title: Backend Persistence Validation Plan
content: This persistence validation plan ensures backend data is durable, accurate, and recoverable through real-world conditions. Goal: verify that acknowledged writes survive restarts, failures, retries, and schema evolution without corruption or silent loss. Coverage begins with data model integrity: enforce primary and foreign keys, unique constraints, not-null rules, and domain validation at both API and database layers. Core CRUD validation: create records with standard and boundary payloads, confirm immediate and eventual reads, update selected fields without unintended mutation, and delete according to soft-delete or hard-delete policy. Transaction reliability: test multi-step writes inside transactions, force failures between steps, and confirm full rollback with no partial state. Idempotency: resend identical create/update requests using the same idempotency key and verify exactly-once effect. Concurrency: execute parallel updates on the same entity, validate optimistic version checks or lock behavior, and ensure no lost-update anomalies. Restart and crash durability: issue writes, restart app/database, simulate abrupt termination during I/O, then verify all committed data remains present and queryable. Replication and consistency checks: validate read-after-write behavior against replicas, measure replication lag, and prevent stale reads for critical paths through routing rules. Migration safety: run forward migrations on realistic data volume, verify compatibility for old and new records, and rehearse rollback with no truncation or type-loss. Backup and restore drills: create backups, restore to isolated environment, compare checksums/counts, and run application smoke tests against restored data. Observability: require structured mutation logs, correlation IDs, persistence error metrics, deadlock/timeout counters, and alerting thresholds. Security and compliance tie-in: confirm encryption at rest/in transit, redact sensitive fields in logs, and preserve audit trails for regulated entities. Exit criteria: zero critical integrity defects, documented recovery time and recovery point results within targets, and all open risks assigned owners with remediation deadlines.
